(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix provided-cred chain ordering and reduce edge crossings

Provided-cred chain detection was fragile — parsed node labels to
find hosts. Now tracks hostsViaProvidedCred directly during access
node creation when matching against a provided credential. This
ensures attacker->cred->access->host->downstream ordering instead
of cred appearing as a sibling of the host.

Also broadened cred->access matching to check access.method for
the credential username (catches cases where username formats
differ between cred and access records).

Added barycenter heuristic for node ordering within layers (3
passes). Nodes in each layer are sorted by the average Y position
of their parent nodes, which reduces edge crossings — e.g., the
DC->gMSA_ADCS edge no longer has to route through credential boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix kill-chain ordering: provided creds flow attacker->cred->access->host

When a credential is provided (source matches "pentest", "provided",
etc.), the chain now flows: Attacker -> cred -> access -> host ->
downstream findings. Previously the host got a direct edge from
Attacker, making the cred appear as a sibling instead of an ancestor.

Changes:
- Skip attacker->host edge for hosts reachable via provided-cred chain
- Add access->host bridge edges so downstream nodes (vulns, pivots,
  blocked) that connect from the host node stay in the chain

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Prune dead-end vulns from graph, link provided creds from Attacker

Graph now only shows vulns that chain into something downstream
(credential, access). Dead-end findings that didn't lead anywhere
are pruned from the graph but remain in the Vulns table.

Credentials whose source matches "provided", "scope", "pentest",
"given", "initial", or "pre-engagement" now link from the Attacker
node instead of from a host — reflecting that they were supplied
as part of scope, not discovered through exploitation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Render pivots as labeled edges instead of nodes

Pivots are relationships, not entities — they now render as styled
edges between source access/host and destination host. Edge label
shows method + status (e.g. "sshuttle", "chisel (identified)").
Exploited pivots are solid green, identified are dashed yellow,
blocked are dotted red. Removes the uninformative column of
identical "pivot" boxes.

Edge legend labels updated: Exploited/Identified/Blocked.

commit.message:
Improve kill-chain graph: subtitles, legend, manual refresh

Graph nodes now show two lines — label + contextual subtitle:
- Hosts: IP + role/OS
- Vulns: title + severity (color-coded by severity level)
- Credentials: user + secret_type (cracked)
- Access: user@host + type|privilege
- Pivots: method + source->destination (instead of generic "pivot")
- Blocked: technique + truncated reason

Adds a legend bar below the graph showing node shapes/colors and
edge styles (confirmed/pending/blocked).

SSE auto-refresh now only updates cards + tables. Graph only redraws
on explicit Refresh button click to prevent disruptive reloads.

commit.message:
Add state-viewer web dashboard with kill-chain graph

Read-only browser dashboard for engagement/state.db served at localhost:8099.
Stdlib-only Python HTTP server with inline HTML/CSS/JS — no dependencies.

Features:
- Kill-chain SVG attack graph (hosts, vulns, creds, access, pivots, blocked)
- State tables with sorting, filtering, collapsible sections
- SSE live updates (events every 2s, full refresh every 10s)
- Token-based auth for remote access (generate-token.sh binds 0.0.0.0)
- HMAC-signed session cookies, Bearer header support for API clients
- Graceful degradation when DB doesn't exist yet

Closes #64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: AI剧本解析时强调场景细分和镜头时长

在AI解析剧本时添加两个重要的强调点：
1. 对场景数量、镜头数量进行最大程度的细分（宁可多分不可少分）
2. 每个镜头要有足够的叙事时长（建议至少3-5秒）

这将确保AI生成的剧本具有更丰富的场景划分和更完整的叙事节奏。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(eval_log_stripper): add s3:PutObjectTagging IAM permission (#961)

## Summary

- Adds `s3:PutObjectTagging` and `s3:GetObjectTagging` permissions to
the eval_log_stripper batch job IAM role, fixing `AccessDenied` errors
on upload
- Copies `InspectModels` tag from source `.eval` to output `.fast.eval`
file so model access control is preserved on stripped files

## Context

The eval_log_stripper uploads `.fast.eval` files with S3 object tags
(`inspect-ai:skip-import=true`) via `ExtraArgs={"Tagging": ...}`, but
the IAM role was missing `s3:PutObjectTagging`. This caused all strip
jobs to fail with AccessDenied.

Sentry: https://metr-sh.sentry.io/issues/HAWK-3PR

## Test plan

- [x] All 30 eval_log_stripper unit tests pass
- [ ] Deploy terraform to staging and verify stripper jobs succeed
- [ ] Verify `.fast.eval` files have correct `InspectModels` tag

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Make terrain renderer projection-aware (centered Mercator + Globe) (#62)

The terrain renderer previously ignored the projection system entirely,
always rendering in flat standard Mercator space. This caused terrain
meshes to be misaligned with raster tiles in centered Mercator and Globe
projection modes.

Changes:
- Add build_terrain_mesh_centered(): terrain mesh in oblique Mercator
  space with elevation, matching raster tile_centered_mesh() positioning
- Add build_terrain_mesh_globe(): terrain mesh on unit sphere with
  radial elevation displacement, matching raster tile_globe_mesh()
- TerrainRenderer::render_terrain_layered() now accepts ProjectionMode
  and uses the correct VP matrix, tile center, and mesh builder per mode
- Mesh cache invalidated on projection change; centered Mercator meshes
  rebuilt every frame (oblique center changes with viewport pan)
- Remove unused to_terrain_view_proj_f64() (superseded by
  to_view_proj_f64_projected(mode))
- Update native and web render loops to pass projection mode

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Hk4CzbMgpv4Q9oXWAcjGqr

Fix: use MapInboundClaims=false to preserve JWT 'sub' claim in .NET 9

In .NET 8+, AddJwtBearer uses JsonWebTokenHandler which ignores
JwtSecurityTokenHandler.DefaultInboundClaimTypeMap. The correct fix is
options.MapInboundClaims = false, which prevents 'sub' from being remapped
to ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier, allowing CurrentUserService to read UserId.

  all lookup tables complete, legal basis cited for every rule

commit.message:
feat: add security headers middleware and rate limiting to publish/auth endpoints (#230)

Add SecurityHeadersMiddleware (raw ASGI) that injects X-Frame-Options: DENY,
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, and Strict-Transport-Security on every
response. Add per-IP sliding-window rate limiters (10 req/min) to
POST /v1/publish, POST /auth/github/code, and POST /auth/github/token.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(acp): use streaming for ACP responses and keep subscription handles alive

ACP servers deliver content via session/update streaming notifications,
not in the final session/prompt response. Two bugs were causing empty
responses:

1. AcpAdapter::generate() used chat() which only reads the final
   response (empty content). Now uses generate_stream() to collect
   streaming deltas.

2. generate_stream() stored SubscriptionHandle as a local variable
   that was dropped on return, killing the notification handler before
   data arrived. AcpStream now has a keep_alive() method to hold
   handles for the stream's lifetime.

Also sets PermissionPolicy::AutoApprove on all server connections to
prevent tool-use hangs from the default Prompt policy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Pass auto-detected server IP from host to container

Container-side IP detection would return the container's internal IP
(172.17.x.x), not the host's public IP. This breaks ArmorChat connectivity.

Changes:
- Detect server IP on host before running container
- Pass detected IP via ARMORCLAW_SERVER_NAME env var
- Works for both full stack and bridge-only modes
- Display detected IP to user during setup

This ensures ArmorChat can connect to the correct server address.

commit.message:
feat(doom-overlay): add download progress callbacks, abort timeout, and
improved error reporting to WAD file finder

Improve the doom-overlay test extension's WAD file handling:

wad-finder.ts:
- Add onProgress callback parameter to ensureWadFile() for reporting
  download status back to the extension UI
- Add WAD_DOWNLOAD_TIMEOUT_MS (20s) abort controller to prevent fetch
  from hanging indefinitely on slow/unresponsive mirrors
- Emit progress messages at each stage: checking local, found existing,
  downloading, downloaded with byte count, and download failure with
  error details
- Capture and report the error object on download failure instead of
  silently returning null

index.ts:
- Pass a progress callback to ensureWadFile() that forwards status
  messages through ctx.ui.notify() so the user sees download progress
  in the extension overlay

commit.message:
fix: isReset false positive — 'sair' in long messages triggered session clear

The message "eu vou sair cedinho, mas eu acho que não vou chegar a tempo"
contained 'sair', triggering isReset which cleared the session and replied
"Tudo bem! Quando quiser agendar, é só me chamar." — masking the implicit
cancel detection that should have asked about the appointment.

Fix: only trigger isReset for messages ≤40 chars. Actual reset commands
("sair", "reiniciar", "esquece") are always short; long messages with 'sair'
mid-sentence are conversational, not commands.
